Patch Set 2:

Nikitas, any response to the questions on v1 of the patch?  Why a revert + fixes instead of just fixes on top of the current tree?

Patch Set 2:

I agree with Andreas. It would be better just to apply the fix on the current tree directly instead of a revert

Patch Set 2:

Andreas, I don't know if there is a benefit to doing the revert; I assumed it would be reasonable since the patch to be reverted introduced a bug that can crash the MDT, as Alex has shown in LU-16235. Could we ensure the revert lands along with the next two patches, to avoid introducing the regression that you mentioned? I am ok with fixing the crash in a separate patch and avoiding the revert, fwiw.

Etienne, are we trying to revert the patch due to the bug that it introduces, or is there another reason?

Abandoned

merge with https://review.whamcloud.com/51850 (LU-16235 hsm: get a valid cookie for RAoLU request)

commit.subject: Revert "LU-16235 hsm: check CDT state before adding actions llog"
commit.message:
Revert "LU-16235 hsm: check CDT state before adding actions llog"

This reverts commit fe5706e0c19f96e4f821790004f05ab265002e9d.

The "LU-16235 hsm: check CDT state before adding actions llog" will
be remplaced by the following patches to cover more cases:
- LU-16356 hsm: add running ref to the coordinator
  (https://review.whamcloud.com/51256)
- LU-16235 hsm: get a valid cookie for RAoLU request
  (https://review.whamcloud.com/51850)

Signed-off-by: Etienne AUJAMES <eaujames@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikitas Angelinas <nikitas.angelinas@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I970fcb9a0ef7838de7f06f78f32fcc767080e960
